# Marco Vargas

> Portuguese architect from Ovar. Master's in Architecture from the Faculty of Architecture of the University of Porto (FAUP), 2025. Practice spans architectural design with BIM software, creative consulting, and visual direction, centered on the relationship between space, atmosphere, and identity.

## About

Marco Vargas is a Portuguese architect from Ovar who completed his studies at the Faculty of Architecture of the University of Porto (FAUP) in 2025. His master's thesis, "Villa Mater: a house in Azores", was supervised by Professor and Architect Patrícia da Silva, faculty at the Accademia di Architettura, USI Mendrisio. The project reflects his interest in essential architecture as a space of care, silence, and transformation.

During his education, Marco participated in two editions of Porto Academy, under the direction of Raphael Zuber and Kersten Geers. His contributions to these workshops were published in the program's official editions. In 2016 he was awarded first prize in two competitions: "GO! Architecture" for AEFAUP (a student competition) and the Warsaw Under Construction Festival 8, during his internship with BUDCUD in Kraków. Both proposals focused on the experience of space.

Marco began his professional career as a junior architect at EBEJER BONNICI studio in Malta, contributing to residential and commercial projects in close collaboration with local architecture and engineering teams. Notable works include the renovation of an apartment in a historic building in London, the restoration and renovation of an 18th-century palace in Birkirkara, and the design of three large residences in Naxxar. During his time in Malta he also took on site supervision under his supervisor, deepening direct contact with construction processes and the management of multidisciplinary teams in an international context.

His current practice intersects architecture specialized in BIM software, creative consulting, and visual direction, maintaining an approach centered on the relationship between space, atmosphere, and identity.

## Selected work

- [Villa Mater: a house in Azores](https://hdl.handle.net/10216/166408): Master's thesis, FAUP, 2025. Supervised by Patrícia da Silva (Accademia di Architettura, USI Mendrisio). Available in the U.Porto institutional repository.

## Awards

- 1st prize, GO! Architecture, AEFAUP (2016)
- 1st prize, Warsaw Under Construction Festival 8 (2016, with BUDCUD, Kraków)

## Education

- Master's in Architecture, Faculdade de Arquitectura da Universidade do Porto (FAUP), 2025
- Porto Academy, under Raphael Zuber and Kersten Geers (two editions)

## Experience

- Junior Architect, EBEJER BONNICI, Malta — residential and commercial projects in Malta, London, Birkirkara, Naxxar; site supervision.
- Internship, BUDCUD, Kraków

## Influences and references

Theoretical and conceptual touchstones: "Architectonic Space" (Dom Hans van der Laan), "Iconographic Autobiography" (Valerio Olgiati), "Villa Além" (Olgiati, Alentejo).

Architects whose work informs the practice — grouped by line of thought:

- **Swiss contemporary / essential**: Valerio Olgiati, Peter Zumthor, Peter Märkli, Christian Kerez, Miroslav Šik, Roger Diener, Mario Botta, Hermann Czech.
- **Portuguese school**: Álvaro Siza, Eduardo Souto de Moura, Manuel Aires Mateus, Francisco Aires Mateus.
- **Japanese tradition and modernity**: Kazuo Shinohara, Arata Isozaki, Toyo Ito, Kazuyo Sejima, Ryue Nishizawa, Sou Fujimoto, Junya Ishigami, Go Hasegawa, Tomoaki Uno, Momoyo Kaijima, Yoshiharu Tsukamoto (Atelier Bow-Wow), Bijoy Jain (Studio Mumbai).
- **Modern canon**: Ludwig Mies van der Rohe, Frank Lloyd Wright, Dom Hans van der Laan.
- **Contemporary international**: David Chipperfield, David Adjaye, Alejandro Aravena, Peter Eisenman, Steven Holl, John Pawson, Richard Meier, Glenn Murcutt, Richard Rogers, Alberto Campo Baeza, Ben van Berkel (UNStudio), Hans Kollhoff, Winy Maas (MVRDV), Jürgen Mayer H., Antón García-Abril, Preston Scott Cohen, Anne Holtrop, Cecilia Puga, Smiljan Radić, Adam Caruso and Peter St John (Caruso St John), Jonathan Sergison and Stephen Bates (Sergison Bates), Jacques Herzog and Pierre de Meuron (Herzog & de Meuron), Peter Wilson (Bolles+Wilson), Robert Venturi and Denise Scott Brown.
